thesis: Cerebras designs and manufactures wafer-scale AI compute chips (WSE-3) with a unique moat in massive single-chip integration. The company is transitioning from heavy customer concentration (86% from UAE/G42 in 2025) to a diversified base anchored by a $20B+ OpenAI Master Relationship Agreement and an AWS binding term sheet. 2026 revenue guidance of >$800M (+50%+) and a $24.6B RPO backlog provide multi-year visibility, but contra-revenue accounting and warrant dilution from the OpenAI deal will pressure reported margins in H1 2026. Extreme valuation (~65x sales) and customer concentration risk warrant a Speculative Buy rating.

drivers: OpenAI deal ramps faster than expected; 750MW expands to 2GW
AWS integration drives new enterprise and hyperscaler customers
Contra-revenue and margin pressure prove temporary; gross margins expand in H2 2026
Additional hyperscaler or sovereign AI cloud wins beyond OpenAI/AWS
WSE-3 volume manufacturing yields improve, reducing unit costs

risks: Customer concentration bites — OpenAI delays or reduces orders
Contra-revenue drags reported revenue and margins more than expected
Warrant dilution significantly expands shares outstanding
Competition from NVIDIA GPUs and custom silicon (Broadcom/Marvell) erodes TAM
AI capex cycle softens; hyperscaler spending decelerates

base_case.assumptions: 2026 guidance met; backlog converts steadily; margins stabilize in H2 2026; OpenAI + AWS diversify revenue; no major competitive share loss.

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 earnings — first post-IPO report; EPS est -$0.14, revenue est $56.65M
Q2 2026 earnings — OpenAI ramp visibility and margin trajectory
AWS integration milestones — product availability or customer adoption updates
Potential new customer announcements beyond OpenAI/AWS

key_risks: Customer concentration: historically 86% from UAE/G42; now acute OpenAI dependency
Contra-revenue and warrant accounting diluting reported revenue and margins
Valuation: ~$52B market cap on ~$800M revenue = ~65x sales
Competition from NVIDIA GPUs and custom silicon (Broadcom, Marvell)
TSMC supply chain and geopolitical risk for wafer-scale fabrication
